docs.title: Welcome to Magi Documentation
docs.text:
Magi is a self-hosted, lightweight manga server and reader designed for simplicity, performance, and ease of use. Whether you're managing a small personal collection or a large library, Magi provides all the tools you need to organize, index, and enjoy your digital manga collection.

[!IMPORTANT] Magi does NOT distribute copyrighted material. It's designed exclusively as a local library manager for your legally obtained manga files. Metadata and cover art are fetched from public APIs like MangaDex to enrich your reading experience.

docs.title: What is Magi?
docs.text: Magi is a complete manga management solution that runs as a single binary on your server, desktop, or NAS. It automatically indexes your manga collection, fetches metadata from MangaDex, and provides a beautiful web interface for reading and managing your library.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• 📚 Automatic Library Management: Scan directories and auto-organize your collection
  • 📖 Modern Reader: Multiple reading modes (webtoon, single page, side-by-side)
  • 👥 Multi-User Support: Create accounts with role-based permissions
  • 🔍 Advanced Search: Filter by tags, types, content rating, and more
  • 🎨 Rich Metadata: Automatic cover art, descriptions, and tags from MangaDex
  • ⚡ High Performance: Written in Go with SQLite for speed and efficiency
  • 🐳 Container Ready: Docker images available for easy deployment
  • 📱 Responsive Design: Works on desktop, tablet, and mobile devices

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
Choose your installation method:

=== "Docker" 

docker run -d \
  --name magi \
  -p 3000:3000 \
  -v /path/to/manga:/data/manga \
  -v /path/to/magi-data:/data/magi \
  alexbruun/magi:latest

=== "Binary" 1. Download from GitHub Releases 2. Run: ./magi 3. Open: http://localhost:3000

=== "Docker Compose" 

version: '3.8'
services:
  magi:
    image: alexbruun/magi:latest
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    volumes:
      - /path/to/manga:/data/manga:ro
      - magi-data:/data/magi
    restart: unless-stopped
volumes:
  magi-data:

After starting Magi, navigate to http://localhost:3000 and create your admin account (the first user automatically becomes admin).

docs.title: Platform Support
docs.text:
Magi builds to a single portable binary for:

Platform Architectures Linux amd64, arm64 Windows amd64, arm64

Docker images are available for linux/amd64 and linux/arm64.

docs.title: File Format Support
docs.text:
Magi natively supports common manga archive formats:

  • ✅ CBZ (Comic Book ZIP) - Recommended
  • ✅ CBR (Comic Book RAR)
  • ✅ ZIP archives
  • ✅ RAR archives

[!TIP] Performance Tip: CBZ/ZIP files offer better random access performance than RAR. For the best experience, consider converting RAR files to ZIP format.

docs.title: Architecture Overview
docs.text:
Magi is built with cutting-edge web technologies:

  • Backend: Go + Fiber (Express-inspired web framework)
  • Database: SQLite with automatic migrations
  • Templates: Templ (type-safe HTML generation)
  • Frontend: HTMX + Franken UI (minimal JavaScript)
  • Metadata: MangaDex API (no authentication required)

Everything compiles into a single binary with embedded assets—no dependencies required.

docs.title: Docker Installation Guide
docs.text: Docker is the recommended way to run Magi. It provides isolation, easy updates, and consistent behavior across platforms.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Docker Engine 20.10+ or Docker Desktop
  • At least 512MB of available RAM
  • Storage for your manga collection and Magi database

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
Pull and run Magi with a single command:

docker run -d \
  --name magi \
  -p 3000:3000 \
  -v /path/to/manga:/data/manga:ro \
  -v magi-data:/data/magi \
  alexbruun/magi:latest

Replace /path/to/manga with the actual path to your manga collection.

docs.title: Docker Compose (Recommended)
docs.text: For production use, Docker Compose provides easier management and persistence.

docs.title: Basic Configuration
docs.text:
Create a docker-compose.yml file:

version: '3.8'

services:
  magi:
    image: alexbruun/magi:latest
    container_name: magi
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    volumes:
      # Mount your manga collection (read-only recommended)
      - /path/to/manga:/data/manga:ro
      # Persistent data for database and cache
      - magi-data:/data/magi
    environment:
      - MAGI_DATA_DIR=/data/magi
      - PORT=3000
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  magi-data:

docs.title: Advanced Configuration
docs.text:
For multiple manga libraries or custom settings:

version: '3.8'

services:
  magi:
    image: alexbruun/magi:latest
    container_name: magi
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    volumes:
      # Multiple manga collections
      - /path/to/manga/library1:/data/manga/library1:ro
      - /path/to/manga/library2:/data/manga/library2:ro
      - /mnt/nas/manga:/data/manga/nas:ro
      # Persistent application data
      - magi-data:/data/magi
    environment:
      - MAGI_DATA_DIR=/data/magi
      - PORT=3000
      - TZ=America/New_York  # Set your timezone
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--quiet", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:3000"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 40s

volumes:
  magi-data:

docs.title: Starting the Container
docs.text:
# Start in foreground (see logs)
docker compose up

# Start in background (detached)
docker compose up -d

# View logs
docker compose logs -f

# Stop the container
docker compose down

docs.title: Required Volumes
docs.text: Path in Container Purpose Recommended Mount /data/magi Database and application data Named volume /data/manga Manga collection Bind mount (read-only)

docs.title: Mounting Your Manga Collection
docs.text:
Linux: 

-v /home/user/manga:/data/manga:ro

Windows (PowerShell): 

-v C:\Users\YourName\Manga:/data/manga:ro

Windows (CMD): 

-v C:\Users\YourName\Manga:/data/manga:ro

NAS/Network Share: 

-v /mnt/nas/manga:/data/manga:ro

[!TIP] The :ro flag mounts the volume as read-only, preventing Magi from accidentally modifying your manga files.

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Configure Magi's behavior with environment variables:

Variable Default Description MAGI_DATA_DIR /data/magi Directory for database and cache PORT 3000 HTTP server port TZ UTC Timezone for cron jobs and logs

Example with custom port:

docker run -d \
  --name magi \
  -p 8080:8080 \
  -e PORT=8080 \
  -v /path/to/manga:/data/manga:ro \
  -v magi-data:/data/magi \
  alexbruun/magi:latest

docs.title: With Docker Compose
docs.text:
# Pull the latest image
docker compose pull

# Recreate the container
docker compose up -d

# Remove old images
docker image prune -f

docs.title: With Docker CLI
docs.text:
# Stop and remove the old container
docker stop magi
docker rm magi

# Pull the latest image
docker pull alexbruun/magi:latest

# Start a new container with the same settings
docker run -d \
  --name magi \
  -p 3000:3000 \
  -v /path/to/manga:/data/manga:ro \
  -v magi-data:/data/magi \
  alexbruun/magi:latest

docs.title: Image Tags
docs.text: Tag Description latest Latest stable release (recommended) v1.2.3 Specific version develop Development build (may be unstable)

docs.title: Verification
docs.text:
After starting the container, verify it's running correctly:

# Check container status
docker ps | grep magi

# View logs
docker logs magi

# Check health (if healthcheck configured)
docker inspect --format='{{.State.Health.Status}}' magi

Then open http://localhost:3000 in your browser. You should see the Magi login page.

docs.title: Container Won't Start
docs.text:
Check logs: 

docker logs magi

Common issues: - Port 3000 already in use → Change to another port: -p 8080:3000 - Volume mount paths incorrect → Verify paths exist and are readable - Insufficient permissions → Ensure Docker has access to mounted directories

docs.title: Can't Access Web Interface
docs.text:
1. Verify container is running: docker ps
  2. Check port mapping: docker port magi
  3. Test from inside container: docker exec magi wget -qO- http://localhost:3000
  4. Check firewall rules on host

docs.title: Database Issues
docs.text:
Reset database (WARNING: Deletes all data): 

docker compose down
docker volume rm magi_magi-data
docker compose up -d

docs.title: Permission Errors
docs.text:
If Magi can't read your manga files:

# Check directory permissions
ls -la /path/to/manga

# Fix permissions (Linux)
chmod -R 755 /path/to/manga

docs.title: Kubernetes Deployment Guide
docs.text: Deploy Magi on Kubernetes for high availability and scalability.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Kubernetes cluster (1.19+)
  • kubectl configured to access your cluster
  • Persistent storage provisioner (optional but recommended)
  • At least 512MB RAM per pod

docs.title: Single Command Deployment
docs.text: kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/alexander-bruun/magi/main/k8s/deployment.yaml

docs.title: Method 1: Using Persistent Volume Claim (Recommended)
docs.text: This method uses dynamic provisioning for persistent storage.

docs.title: 1. Create Namespace
docs.text:
# namespace.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Namespace
metadata:
  name: magi

Apply: 

kubectl apply -f namespace.yaml

docs.title: 2. Create Persistent Volume Claim
docs.text:
# pvc.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: magi-data
  namespace: magi
spec:
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 10Gi
  storageClassName: standard  # Change to your storage class

Apply: 

kubectl apply -f pvc.yaml

docs.title: 3. Create Deployment
docs.text:
# deployment.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: magi
  namespace: magi
  labels:
    app: magi
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: magi
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: magi
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: magi
        image: alexbruun/magi:latest
        imagePullPolicy: Always
        ports:
        - containerPort: 3000
          name: http
          protocol: TCP
        env:
        - name: MAGI_DATA_DIR
          value: /data/magi
        - name: PORT
          value: "3000"
        - name: TZ
          value: "UTC"
        volumeMounts:
        - name: magi-data
          mountPath: /data/magi
        - name: manga-storage
          mountPath: /data/manga
          readOnly: true
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "250m"
          limits:
            memory: "2Gi"
            cpu: "1000m"
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /
            port: 3000
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
          timeoutSeconds: 5
          failureThreshold: 3
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /
            port: 3000
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
          timeoutSeconds: 3
          failureThreshold: 3
      volumes:
      - name: magi-data
        persistentVolumeClaim:
          claimName: magi-data
      - name: manga-storage
        hostPath:
          path: /mnt/manga  # Change to your manga path
          type: Directory

Apply: 

kubectl apply -f deployment.yaml

docs.title: 4. Create Service
docs.text:
# service.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: magi
  namespace: magi
  labels:
    app: magi
spec:
  type: ClusterIP
  ports:
  - port: 3000
    targetPort: 3000
    protocol: TCP
    name: http
  selector:
    app: magi

Apply: 

kubectl apply -f service.yaml

docs.title: 5. Create Ingress (Optional)
docs.text:
For external access with a domain name:

# ingress.yaml
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: magi
  namespace: magi
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: "letsencrypt-prod"
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-body-size: "100m"
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx
  tls:
  - hosts:
    - magi.example.com
    secretName: magi-tls
  rules:
  - host: magi.example.com
    http:
      paths:
      - path: /
        pathType: Prefix
        backend:
          service:
            name: magi
            port:
              number: 3000

Apply: 

kubectl apply -f ingress.yaml

docs.title: Method 2: Using HostPath Volumes
docs.text:
Suitable for single-node clusters or testing.

# deployment-hostpath.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: magi
  namespace: magi
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: magi
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: magi
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: magi
        image: alexbruun/magi:latest
        ports:
        - containerPort: 3000
        volumeMounts:
        - name: magi-data
          mountPath: /data/magi
        - name: manga-storage
          mountPath: /data/manga
          readOnly: true
      volumes:
      - name: magi-data
        hostPath:
          path: /mnt/kubernetes/magi
          type: DirectoryOrCreate
      - name: manga-storage
        hostPath:
          path: /mnt/manga
          type: Directory

Apply: 

kubectl apply -f deployment-hostpath.yaml

docs.title: Method 3: Using NFS Storage
docs.text: For shared storage across multiple nodes:

docs.title: 1. Create NFS Persistent Volume
docs.text:
# pv-nfs.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolume
metadata:
  name: magi-nfs-pv
spec:
  capacity:
    storage: 10Gi
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteMany
  nfs:
    server: nfs-server.example.com
    path: /export/magi
  mountOptions:
    - hard
    - nfsvers=4.1

docs.title: 2. Create PVC
docs.text:
# pvc-nfs.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: magi-data
  namespace: magi
spec:
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteMany
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 10Gi
  volumeName: magi-nfs-pv

Apply: 

kubectl apply -f pv-nfs.yaml
kubectl apply -f pvc-nfs.yaml

docs.title: Verification
docs.text:
Check deployment status:

# Check all resources in magi namespace
kubectl get all -n magi

# Check pod logs
kubectl logs -f deployment/magi -n magi

# Check pod details
kubectl describe pod -l app=magi -n magi

# Check persistent volumes
kubectl get pv
kubectl get pvc -n magi

docs.title: Port Forward (Testing)
docs.text:
kubectl port-forward -n magi service/magi 3000:3000

Then access: http://localhost:3000

docs.title: NodePort Service
docs.text:
# service-nodeport.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: magi
  namespace: magi
spec:
  type: NodePort
  ports:
  - port: 3000
    targetPort: 3000
    nodePort: 30000  # Choose port 30000-32767
  selector:
    app: magi

Access via: http://[node-ip]:30000

docs.title: LoadBalancer Service
docs.text:
For cloud providers:

# service-loadbalancer.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: magi
  namespace: magi
spec:
  type: LoadBalancer
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 3000
  selector:
    app: magi

Get external IP: 

kubectl get service magi -n magi

docs.title: ConfigMap for Environment Variables
docs.text:
# configmap.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: magi-config
  namespace: magi
data:
  MAGI_DATA_DIR: "/data/magi"
  PORT: "3000"
  TZ: "America/New_York"

Reference in deployment:

spec:
  containers:
  - name: magi
    envFrom:
    - configMapRef:
        name: magi-config

docs.title: Secrets for Sensitive Data
docs.text:
# secret.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: magi-secrets
  namespace: magi
type: Opaque
stringData:
  admin-password: "change-me"

docs.title: Rolling Update
docs.text:
# Update image to latest
kubectl set image deployment/magi magi=alexbruun/magi:latest -n magi

# Or edit deployment
kubectl edit deployment magi -n magi

# Watch rollout status
kubectl rollout status deployment/magi -n magi

docs.title: Rollback
docs.text:
# View rollout history
kubectl rollout history deployment/magi -n magi

# Rollback to previous version
kubectl rollout undo deployment/magi -n magi

# Rollback to specific revision
kubectl rollout undo deployment/magi --to-revision=2 -n magi

docs.title: Scaling
docs.text:
# Scale to multiple replicas (if using ReadWriteMany storage)
kubectl scale deployment magi --replicas=3 -n magi

# Auto-scaling (HPA)
kubectl autoscale deployment magi \
  --cpu-percent=80 \
  --min=1 \
  --max=5 \
  -n magi

[!WARNING] Magi uses SQLite which doesn't support concurrent writes from multiple pods. Keep replicas=1 unless using a different database backend.

docs.title: Pod Not Starting
docs.text:
# Check pod status
kubectl get pods -n magi

# Describe pod for events
kubectl describe pod -l app=magi -n magi

# Check logs
kubectl logs -f deployment/magi -n magi

# Check previous container logs (if restarting)
kubectl logs deployment/magi -n magi --previous

docs.title: Storage Issues
docs.text:
# Check PVC status
kubectl get pvc -n magi

# Describe PVC
kubectl describe pvc magi-data -n magi

# Check PV
kubectl get pv

docs.title: Network Issues
docs.text:
# Test service connectivity from another pod
kubectl run -it --rm debug --image=busybox --restart=Never -n magi -- wget -qO- http://magi:3000

# Check service endpoints
kubectl get endpoints magi -n magi

docs.title: Resource Usage
docs.text:
# View resource usage
kubectl top pod -n magi
kubectl top node

docs.title: Logs
docs.text:
# Stream logs
kubectl logs -f deployment/magi -n magi

# Last 100 lines
kubectl logs deployment/magi -n magi --tail=100

# Logs from all pods
kubectl logs -l app=magi -n magi --all-containers=true

docs.title: Cleanup
docs.text:
Remove all Magi resources:

# Delete namespace (removes everything)
kubectl delete namespace magi

# Or delete individual resources
kubectl delete deployment magi -n magi
kubectl delete service magi -n magi
kubectl delete pvc magi-data -n magi
kubectl delete pv magi-nfs-pv  # If using NFS

docs.title: Linux Installation Guide
docs.text: This guide covers installing Magi as a native binary on Linux with systemd service management.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Linux distribution with systemd (Ubuntu, Debian, Fedora, CentOS, etc.)
  • 512MB+ available RAM
  • Storage for manga collection and database

docs.title: 1. Download Magi
docs.text:
Download the appropriate binary for your architecture from the releases page.

For x86_64 (amd64): 

wget https://github.com/alexander-bruun/magi/releases/latest/download/magi-linux-amd64
chmod +x magi-linux-amd64
sudo mv magi-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/magi

For ARM64: 

wget https://github.com/alexander-bruun/magi/releases/latest/download/magi-linux-arm64
chmod +x magi-linux-arm64
sudo mv magi-linux-arm64 /usr/local/bin/magi

docs.title: 2. Create a Dedicated User (Optional but Recommended)
docs.text:
Running Magi as a dedicated user improves security:

sudo useradd --system --no-create-home --shell /bin/false magi

docs.title: 3. Create Data Directory
docs.text:
sudo mkdir -p /var/lib/magi
sudo chown magi:magi /var/lib/magi

docs.title: 4. Create Systemd Service File
docs.text:
Create /etc/systemd/system/magi.service:

sudo nano /etc/systemd/system/magi.service

Basic service configuration:

[Unit]
Description=Magi Manga Server
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=magi
Group=magi
WorkingDirectory=/var/lib/magi
Environment="MAGI_DATA_DIR=/var/lib/magi"
Environment="PORT=3000"
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/magi
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=5s

# Security hardening
NoNewPrivileges=true
PrivateTmp=true
ProtectSystem=strict
ProtectHome=true
ReadWritePaths=/var/lib/magi
ReadOnlyPaths=/path/to/manga

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

Advanced service configuration with better security:

[Unit]
Description=Magi Manga Server
Documentation=https://alexander-bruun.github.io/magi/
After=network-online.target
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=simple
User=magi
Group=magi
WorkingDirectory=/var/lib/magi

# Environment variables
Environment="MAGI_DATA_DIR=/var/lib/magi"
Environment="PORT=3000"

# Main process
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/magi
ExecReload=/bin/kill -HUP $MAINPID

# Restart policy
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=5s
TimeoutStopSec=30s

# Logging
StandardOutput=journal
StandardError=journal
SyslogIdentifier=magi

# Security hardening
NoNewPrivileges=true
PrivateTmp=true
ProtectSystem=strict
ProtectHome=true
ReadWritePaths=/var/lib/magi
ReadOnlyPaths=/path/to/manga

# Additional sandboxing
ProtectKernelTunables=true
ProtectKernelModules=true
ProtectControlGroups=true
RestrictNamespaces=true
RestrictRealtime=true
RestrictSUIDSGID=true
LockPersonality=true
PrivateDevices=true

# Limit resources
LimitNOFILE=65536
MemoryLimit=2G

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

[!IMPORTANT] Replace /path/to/manga with the actual path to your manga collection.

docs.title: 5. Enable and Start Service
docs.text:
# Reload systemd to recognize the new service
sudo systemctl daemon-reload

# Enable service to start on boot
sudo systemctl enable magi

# Start the service
sudo systemctl start magi

# Check status
sudo systemctl status magi

docs.title: 6. Verify Installation
docs.text:
Check that Magi is running:

# View service status
sudo systemctl status magi

# View logs
sudo journalctl -u magi -f

# Test HTTP endpoint
curl http://localhost:3000

Open http://localhost:3000 in your browser to access Magi.

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Edit the service file to customize Magi's behavior:

sudo systemctl edit --full magi

Add or modify environment variables:

Environment="MAGI_DATA_DIR=/var/lib/magi"
Environment="PORT=3000"
Environment="TZ=America/New_York"

After editing, reload and restart:

sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl restart magi

docs.title: File Permissions
docs.text:
Ensure Magi can read your manga collection:

# Grant read access to manga directory
sudo chmod -R 755 /path/to/manga

# Allow magi user to read manga files
sudo setfacl -R -m u:magi:rx /path/to/manga

docs.title: Common Commands
docs.text:
# Start Magi
sudo systemctl start magi

# Stop Magi
sudo systemctl stop magi

# Restart Magi
sudo systemctl restart magi

# View status
sudo systemctl status magi

# Enable auto-start on boot
sudo systemctl enable magi

# Disable auto-start
sudo systemctl disable magi

# View logs
sudo journalctl -u magi -f

# View last 100 log lines
sudo journalctl -u magi -n 100

# View logs since yesterday
sudo journalctl -u magi --since yesterday

docs.title: Updating Magi
docs.text:
To update to a new version:

# Stop the service
sudo systemctl stop magi

# Download new version
wget https://github.com/alexander-bruun/magi/releases/latest/download/magi-linux-amd64
chmod +x magi-linux-amd64

# Replace old binary
sudo mv magi-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/magi

# Start the service
sudo systemctl start magi

# Verify it's running
sudo systemctl status magi

docs.title: Nginx
docs.text:
If you want to expose Magi behind Nginx:

server {
    listen 80;
    server_name magi.example.com;

    location / {
        proxy_pass http://localhost:3000;
        proxy_http_version 1.1;
        proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
        proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade';
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
        proxy_cache_bypass $http_upgrade;
    }
}

docs.title: Apache
docs.text:
For Apache with mod_proxy:

<VirtualHost *:80>
    ServerName magi.example.com

    ProxyPreserveHost On
    ProxyPass / http://localhost:3000/
    ProxyPassReverse / http://localhost:3000/

    <Location />
        Require all granted
    </Location>
</VirtualHost>

docs.title: Caddy
docs.text:
Caddy provides automatic HTTPS:

magi.example.com {
    reverse_proxy localhost:3000
}

docs.title: Service Won't Start
docs.text:
Check logs: 

sudo journalctl -u magi -n 50 --no-pager

Common issues: - Port already in use → Change PORT in service file - Permission denied → Check file permissions and user - Binary not found → Verify path in ExecStart

docs.title: Permission Errors
docs.text:
If Magi can't access manga files:

# Check current permissions
ls -la /path/to/manga

# Fix ownership (if files owned by another user)
sudo chown -R magi:magi /var/lib/magi

# Grant read access using ACLs
sudo setfacl -R -m u:magi:rx /path/to/manga

docs.title: Database Issues
docs.text:
Reset database (WARNING: Deletes all data): 

sudo systemctl stop magi
sudo rm -rf /var/lib/magi/magi.db
sudo systemctl start magi

docs.title: High Memory Usage
docs.text:
Limit memory in service file:

[Service]
MemoryMax=1G
MemoryHigh=800M

Then reload: 

sudo systemctl daemon-reload
sudo systemctl restart magi

docs.title: Uninstalling
docs.text:
To completely remove Magi:

# Stop and disable service
sudo systemctl stop magi
sudo systemctl disable magi

# Remove service file
sudo rm /etc/systemd/system/magi.service

# Remove binary
sudo rm /usr/local/bin/magi

# Remove data (optional - this deletes your database)
sudo rm -rf /var/lib/magi

# Remove user (optional)
sudo userdel magi

# Reload systemd
sudo systemctl daemon-reload

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Create your first library
  • Configure automatic indexing
  • Set up user accounts

docs.title: Podman Installation Guide
docs.text: Podman is a daemonless container engine that's compatible with Docker. This guide covers installing and running Magi with Podman.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Podman 3.0+ installed
  • 512MB+ available RAM
  • Storage for manga collection and database

docs.title: Fedora/RHEL/CentOS
docs.text: sudo dnf install podman

docs.title: Ubuntu/Debian
docs.text:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install podman

docs.title: Windows
docs.text:
Download from Podman Desktop or use:

winget install RedHat.Podman

docs.title: Basic Run Command
docs.text:
podman run -d \
  --name magi \
  -p 3000:3000 \
  -v /path/to/manga:/data/manga:ro,Z \
  -v magi-data:/data/magi:Z \
  alexbruun/magi:latest

Replace /path/to/manga with your actual manga directory.

[!NOTE] The :Z flag is important for SELinux systems (Fedora, RHEL, CentOS). It relabels the volume for container access.

docs.title: Installing Podman Compose
docs.text:
# Using pip
pip3 install podman-compose

# Or using system packages (Fedora/RHEL)
sudo dnf install podman-compose

docs.title: Basic Configuration
docs.text:
Create docker-compose.yml (compatible with Podman):

version: '3.8'

services:
  magi:
    image: alexbruun/magi:latest
    container_name: magi
    ports:
      - "3000:3000"
    volumes:
      - /path/to/manga:/data/manga:ro
      - magi-data:/data/magi
    environment:
      - MAGI_DATA_DIR=/data/magi
      - PORT=3000
    restart: unless-stopped

volumes:
  magi-data:

docs.title: Running with Podman Compose
docs.text:
# Start services
podman-compose up -d

# View logs
podman-compose logs -f

# Stop services
podman-compose down

docs.title: Rootless Podman (Recommended)
docs.text: Podman can run containers as a regular user without root privileges.

docs.title: Setup Rootless
docs.text:
# Enable user namespaces (if needed)
echo "user.max_user_namespaces=28633" | sudo tee -a /etc/sysctl.conf
sudo sysctl -p

# Verify rootless mode works
podman run --rm hello-world

docs.title: Run Magi Rootless
docs.text:
podman run -d \
  --name magi \
  -p 3000:3000 \
  -v ~/manga:/data/manga:ro \
  -v magi-data:/data/magi \
  alexbruun/magi:latest

docs.title: Systemd Integration
docs.text: Run Magi as a systemd service for automatic startup.

docs.title: Generate Systemd Unit
docs.text:
# Create the container first
podman run -d \
  --name magi \
  -p 3000:3000 \
  -v /path/to/manga:/data/manga:ro,Z \
  -v magi-data:/data/magi:Z \
  alexbruun/magi:latest

# Generate systemd unit file
podman generate systemd --new --name magi > ~/.config/systemd/user/magi.service

# Stop the temporary container
podman stop magi
podman rm magi

docs.title: Enable and Start Service
docs.text:
# Reload systemd
systemctl --user daemon-reload

# Enable service to start on boot
systemctl --user enable magi

# Start service
systemctl --user start magi

# Check status
systemctl --user status magi

# View logs
journalctl --user -u magi -f

docs.title: Enable Linger (Persist After Logout)
docs.text:
# Allow user services to run after logout
sudo loginctl enable-linger $USER

docs.title: Pods (Multi-Container Setup)
docs.text: Podman pods group containers with shared networking.

docs.title: Create a Pod
docs.text:
# Create pod with exposed port
podman pod create \
  --name magi-pod \
  -p 3000:3000

# Run Magi in the pod
podman run -d \
  --pod magi-pod \
  --name magi \
  -v /path/to/manga:/data/manga:ro,Z \
  -v magi-data:/data/magi:Z \
  alexbruun/magi:latest

# Add additional containers if needed (e.g., reverse proxy)

docs.title: Named Volumes
docs.text:
# Create volume
podman volume create magi-data

# Inspect volume
podman volume inspect magi-data

# List volumes
podman volume ls

# Remove volume
podman volume rm magi-data

docs.title: Bind Mounts
docs.text:
# Mount specific directory
podman run -d \
  --name magi \
  -v /home/user/manga:/data/manga:ro,Z \
  -v /home/user/magi-data:/data/magi:Z \
  alexbruun/magi:latest

docs.title: Port Mapping
docs.text:
# Map to different host port
podman run -d --name magi -p 8080:3000 alexbruun/magi:latest

# Bind to specific interface
podman run -d --name magi -p 192.168.1.100:3000:3000 alexbruun/magi:latest

# Map multiple ports
podman run -d --name magi -p 3000:3000 -p 3001:3001 alexbruun/magi:latest

docs.title: Custom Networks
docs.text:
# Create network
podman network create magi-net

# Run container on custom network
podman run -d \
  --name magi \
  --network magi-net \
  alexbruun/magi:latest

docs.title: Basic Commands
docs.text:
# List running containers
podman ps

# List all containers
podman ps -a

# View logs
podman logs magi
podman logs -f magi  # Follow

# Stop container
podman stop magi

# Start container
podman start magi

# Restart container
podman restart magi

# Remove container
podman rm magi

# Execute command in container
podman exec -it magi /bin/sh

docs.title: Resource Limits
docs.text:
podman run -d \
  --name magi \
  --memory=2g \
  --cpus=2 \
  -p 3000:3000 \
  -v /path/to/manga:/data/manga:ro,Z \
  -v magi-data:/data/magi:Z \
  alexbruun/magi:latest

docs.title: Manual Update
docs.text:
# Stop and remove old container
podman stop magi
podman rm magi

# Pull latest image
podman pull alexbruun/magi:latest

# Start new container
podman run -d \
  --name magi \
  -p 3000:3000 \
  -v /path/to/manga:/data/manga:ro,Z \
  -v magi-data:/data/magi:Z \
  alexbruun/magi:latest

docs.title: With Systemd
docs.text:
# Pull new image
podman pull alexbruun/magi:latest

# Restart service (will use new image if --new flag was used)
systemctl --user restart magi

docs.title: Auto-Update
docs.text:
Configure automatic image updates:

# Run with auto-update label
podman run -d \
  --name magi \
  --label "io.containers.autoupdate=registry" \
  -p 3000:3000 \
  -v /path/to/manga:/data/manga:ro,Z \
  -v magi-data:/data/magi:Z \
  alexbruun/magi:latest

# Enable auto-update timer
systemctl --user enable --now podman-auto-update.timer

# Check update status
systemctl --user status podman-auto-update.timer

# Manually trigger update
podman auto-update

docs.title: SELinux Issues
docs.text:
If you get permission denied errors on SELinux systems:

# Use Z flag for private volume labeling
-v /path:/container/path:Z

# Or z flag for shared volume labeling
-v /path:/container/path:z

# Check SELinux context
ls -lZ /path/to/manga

# Restore SELinux context
sudo restorecon -Rv /path/to/manga

docs.title: Port Already in Use
docs.text:
# Find process using port
sudo ss -tulpn | grep 3000

# Use different port
podman run -d --name magi -p 8080:3000 alexbruun/magi:latest

docs.title: Permission Errors
docs.text:
# Check file ownership
ls -la /path/to/manga

# Fix permissions
chmod -R 755 /path/to/manga

# For rootless, ensure files are readable by your user
chown -R $USER:$USER /path/to/manga

docs.title: Container Won't Start
docs.text:
# Check logs
podman logs magi

# Inspect container
podman inspect magi

# Check events
podman events --filter container=magi

docs.title: Podman vs Docker Differences
docs.text:
Feature Docker Podman Daemon Required Daemonless Root Usually needs root Rootless by default Compatibility Docker-specific OCI-compliant Systemd External tools Built-in integration Pods Docker Compose Native pods support

Most Docker commands work with Podman by replacing docker with podman.

docs.title: Using podman-docker Package
docs.text:
# Install Docker compatibility
sudo dnf install podman-docker

# Now 'docker' command uses Podman
docker ps  # Actually runs podman ps

docs.title: Manual Alias
docs.text:
# Add to ~/.bashrc or ~/.zshrc
alias docker=podman

# Reload shell
source ~/.bashrc

docs.title: Verification
docs.text:
After starting Magi, verify it's working:

# Check container status
podman ps | grep magi

# Test HTTP endpoint
curl http://localhost:3000

# Check resource usage
podman stats magi

Then open http://localhost:3000 in your browser.

docs.title: Cleanup
docs.text:
Remove all Magi resources:

# Stop and remove container
podman stop magi
podman rm magi

# Remove volumes (WARNING: Deletes data)
podman volume rm magi-data

# Remove images
podman rmi alexbruun/magi:latest

# Remove pod (if used)
podman pod rm -f magi-pod

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Create your first library
  • Configure automatic indexing
  • Learn about systemd integration

docs.title: Additional Resources
docs.text:
• Podman Documentation
  • Podman Desktop
  • Red Hat Podman Guide

docs.title: Windows Installation Guide
docs.text: This guide covers installing Magi on Windows as a native application or Windows Service.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Windows 10/11 or Windows Server 2016+
  • 512MB+ available RAM
  • Storage for manga collection and database

docs.title: 1. Download Magi
docs.text:
Download the Windows binary from the releases page:

  • For 64-bit Windows: magi-windows-amd64.exe
  • For ARM64 Windows: magi-windows-arm64.exe

docs.title: 2. Create a Directory
docs.text:
Create a folder for Magi:

mkdir C:\Magi
mv Downloads\magi-windows-amd64.exe C:\Magi\magi.exe

docs.title: 3. Run Magi
docs.text:
Open PowerShell or Command Prompt and run:

cd C:\Magi
.\magi.exe

Magi will start and be accessible at http://localhost:3000.

[!TIP] Keep the terminal window open while using Magi. Closing it will stop the server.

docs.title: Method 2: Windows Service with NSSM (Recommended)
docs.text: Running Magi as a Windows Service allows it to start automatically and run in the background.

docs.title: 1. Download NSSM
docs.text:
NSSM (Non-Sucking Service Manager) is a free tool for creating Windows services.

  1. Download NSSM from nssm.cc/download
  2. Extract to C:\NSSM

docs.title: 2. Download Magi
docs.text: Download the Windows binary to C:\Magi\magi.exe.

docs.title: 3. Create Data Directory
docs.text: mkdir C:\MagiData

docs.title: 4. Install as Service
docs.text:
Open PowerShell as Administrator:

cd C:\NSSM\win64

# Install Magi as a service
.\nssm.exe install Magi C:\Magi\magi.exe

# Set working directory
.\nssm.exe set Magi AppDirectory C:\MagiData

# Set environment variables
.\nssm.exe set Magi AppEnvironmentExtra MAGI_DATA_DIR=C:\MagiData

# Configure service to start automatically
.\nssm.exe set Magi Start SERVICE_AUTO_START

# Set service description
.\nssm.exe set Magi Description "Magi Manga Server"

# Configure service recovery on failure
.\nssm.exe set Magi AppRestartDelay 5000

# Start the service
.\nssm.exe start Magi

docs.title: 5. Verify Service
docs.text:
Check service status in Services management console:

  1. Press Win + R
  2. Type services.msc
  3. Look for "Magi" in the list

Or use PowerShell:

Get-Service Magi

docs.title: Method 3: Windows Service with Shawl
docs.text: Shawl is an alternative service wrapper.

docs.title: 1. Install Shawl
docs.text:
Download from GitHub releases:

# Using winget (Windows Package Manager)
winget install shawl

# Or using Scoop
scoop install shawl

docs.title: 2. Install Magi as Service
docs.text:
Open PowerShell as Administrator:

shawl add `
  --name Magi `
  --cwd C:\MagiData `
  --env MAGI_DATA_DIR=C:\MagiData `
  --restart-if-crashed `
  -- C:\Magi\magi.exe

# Start the service
sc.exe start Magi

docs.title: For Direct Execution
docs.text:
Set environment variables before running:

$env:MAGI_DATA_DIR = "C:\MagiData"
$env:PORT = "3000"
.\magi.exe

docs.title: For NSSM Service
docs.text:
.\nssm.exe set Magi AppEnvironmentExtra MAGI_DATA_DIR=C:\MagiData PORT=3000
.\nssm.exe restart Magi

docs.title: For Shawl Service
docs.text:
Edit the service:

shawl edit Magi

docs.title: Custom Port
docs.text:
To use a different port:

# NSSM
.\nssm.exe set Magi AppEnvironmentExtra PORT=8080
.\nssm.exe restart Magi

# Shawl (edit service configuration)
shawl edit Magi

docs.title: Manga Library Paths
docs.text:
Magi will scan directories you configure through the web interface. Common Windows paths:

  • Local drive: C:\Manga
  • Network share: \\nas\Manga
  • External drive: E:\Manga

[!IMPORTANT] When running as a service, ensure the SYSTEM account (or the user running the service) has read access to manga directories.

docs.title: NSSM Commands
docs.text:
# Check service status
.\nssm.exe status Magi

# Start service
.\nssm.exe start Magi

# Stop service
.\nssm.exe stop Magi

# Restart service
.\nssm.exe restart Magi

# View service configuration
.\nssm.exe dump Magi

# Remove service
.\nssm.exe remove Magi confirm

docs.title: Windows Services Console
docs.text:
1. Press Win + R
  2. Type services.msc
  3. Find "Magi" in the list
  4. Right-click for Start, Stop, Restart, Properties

docs.title: PowerShell Service Commands
docs.text:
# Check status
Get-Service Magi

# Start service
Start-Service Magi

# Stop service
Stop-Service Magi

# Restart service
Restart-Service Magi

# Set to start automatically
Set-Service Magi -StartupType Automatic

docs.title: NSSM Logs
docs.text:
Configure logging:

# Enable stdout/stderr logging
.\nssm.exe set Magi AppStdout C:\MagiData\logs\magi.log
.\nssm.exe set Magi AppStderr C:\MagiData\logs\magi-error.log

# Rotate logs daily
.\nssm.exe set Magi AppRotateFiles 1
.\nssm.exe set Magi AppRotateOnline 1
.\nssm.exe set Magi AppRotateSeconds 86400

# Restart service to apply
.\nssm.exe restart Magi

View logs:

# View latest logs
Get-Content C:\MagiData\logs\magi.log -Tail 50

# Follow logs in real-time
Get-Content C:\MagiData\logs\magi.log -Wait

docs.title: Windows Event Viewer
docs.text:
1. Press Win + R
  2. Type eventvwr.msc
  3. Navigate to: Windows Logs > Application
  4. Filter by source "Magi" or "NSSM"

docs.title: Firewall Configuration
docs.text:
Allow Magi through Windows Firewall:

# Add firewall rule (run as Administrator)
New-NetFirewallRule `
  -DisplayName "Magi Manga Server" `
  -Direction Inbound `
  -Protocol TCP `
  -LocalPort 3000 `
  -Action Allow `
  -Description "Allow inbound HTTP traffic to Magi"

Or use Windows Defender Firewall GUI:

  1. Open Windows Defender Firewall with Advanced Security
  2. Click Inbound Rules
  3. Click New Rule
  4. Select Port, click Next
  5. Select TCP, enter port 3000, click Next
  6. Select Allow the connection, click Next
  7. Apply to all profiles, click Next
  8. Name it "Magi Manga Server", click Finish

docs.title: For Direct Execution
docs.text:
1. Download the new version
  2. Stop Magi (close terminal)
  3. Replace C:\Magi\magi.exe
  4. Restart Magi

docs.title: For Service (NSSM)
docs.text:
# Stop service
.\nssm.exe stop Magi

# Replace binary
mv Downloads\magi-windows-amd64.exe C:\Magi\magi.exe -Force

# Start service
.\nssm.exe start Magi

docs.title: For Service (Shawl)
docs.text:
# Stop service
sc.exe stop Magi

# Replace binary
mv Downloads\magi-windows-amd64.exe C:\Magi\magi.exe -Force

# Start service
sc.exe start Magi

docs.title: Service Won't Start
docs.text:
Check Event Viewer: 1. Open Event Viewer (eventvwr.msc) 2. Check Application logs for errors

Common issues: - Port 3000 already in use → Change PORT environment variable - Permission denied → Run service as user with proper permissions - Missing dependencies → Ensure .NET Framework or VC++ Redistributable is installed

docs.title: Can't Access from Other Devices
docs.text:
1. Verify Windows Firewall rule is active
  2. Check port forwarding on router (for external access)
  3. Test locally first: http://localhost:3000
  4. Test from same network: http://[PC-IP]:3000

docs.title: Permission Errors Reading Manga Files
docs.text:
If running as SYSTEM account:

# Grant SYSTEM read access to manga folder
icacls "C:\Manga" /grant "SYSTEM:(OI)(CI)R" /T

Or configure service to run as your user:

.\nssm.exe set Magi ObjectName ".\YourUsername" "YourPassword"
.\nssm.exe restart Magi

docs.title: High Memory Usage
docs.text:
Limit memory in NSSM (Windows 10/11 only):

# Create a scheduled task with memory limit instead

docs.title: Accessing from Other Devices
docs.text:
On your network, access Magi from other devices using your PC's IP address:

http://192.168.1.100:3000

Find your IP:

ipconfig | Select-String "IPv4"

docs.title: Remove NSSM Service
docs.text:
# Stop service
.\nssm.exe stop Magi

# Remove service
.\nssm.exe remove Magi confirm

docs.title: Remove Files
docs.text:
# Remove Magi
Remove-Item -Recurse -Force C:\Magi

# Remove data (optional - deletes database)
Remove-Item -Recurse -Force C:\MagiData

# Remove firewall rule
Remove-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName "Magi Manga Server"

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Create your first library
  • Configure automatic indexing
  • Set up user accounts

docs.title: Configuration Guide
docs.text: This guide covers all configuration options available in Magi, from basic settings to advanced customization.

docs.title: Admin Configuration Page
docs.text: Access the configuration page via Admin > Configuration (admin role required).

docs.title: Allow New User Registrations
docs.text:
Controls whether new users can create accounts.

  • Enabled: Registration page is accessible, new users can sign up
  • Disabled: Registration page shows "Registration is disabled" message

Use cases: - Disable after creating accounts for family/friends - Enable temporarily when adding new users - Keep disabled for single-user installations

To change:

  1. Navigate to Admin > Configuration
  2. Toggle "Allow new user registrations"
  3. Click Save

docs.title: Maximum Users
docs.text:
Sets a hard limit on total user accounts.

  • 0: Unlimited users (default)
  • >0: Blocks registration once limit is reached

Use cases: - Limit server resources for personal use - Control access to your manga collection - Prevent unauthorized account creation

Example: 

Max Users: 5

After 5 accounts are created, registration attempts will be rejected even if registration is enabled.

docs.title: First User Privileges
docs.text: The first user to register on a fresh Magi installation automatically receives admin role. This ensures you have full control from the start.

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Configure Magi's behavior with environment variables set before starting the application.

docs.title: MAGI_DATA_DIR
docs.text:
Description: Directory for database and cached data Default: OS-specific: - Linux: ~/.local/share/magi - Windows: %APPDATA%\magi

Example: 

export MAGI_DATA_DIR=/var/lib/magi

docs.title: PORT
docs.text:
Description: HTTP server port Default: 3000

Example: 

export PORT=8080

docs.title: TZ
docs.text:
Description: Timezone for cron jobs and logs Default: System timezone

Example: 

export TZ=America/New_York

Common timezones: - America/New_York - Eastern Time - America/Los_Angeles - Pacific Time - Europe/London - British Time - Asia/Tokyo - Japan Standard Time - Australia/Sydney - Australian Eastern Time

Find your timezone: Wikipedia List

docs.title: Docker
docs.text:
docker run -d \
  -e MAGI_DATA_DIR=/data/magi \
  -e PORT=3000 \
  -e TZ=America/New_York \
  alexbruun/magi:latest

docs.title: Docker Compose
docs.text:
services:
  magi:
    image: alexbruun/magi:latest
    environment:
      - MAGI_DATA_DIR=/data/magi
      - PORT=3000
      - TZ=America/New_York

docs.title: Linux Systemd
docs.text:
Edit /etc/systemd/system/magi.service:

[Service]
Environment="MAGI_DATA_DIR=/var/lib/magi"
Environment="PORT=3000"
Environment="TZ=America/New_York"

docs.title: Windows (PowerShell)
docs.text:
$env:MAGI_DATA_DIR = "C:\MagiData"
$env:PORT = "3000"
.\magi.exe

docs.title: Windows Service (NSSM)
docs.text: .\nssm.exe set Magi AppEnvironmentExtra MAGI_DATA_DIR=C:\MagiData PORT=3000

docs.title: Library Configuration
docs.text: Manage libraries from Admin > Libraries.

docs.title: Creating a Library
docs.text:
1. Click New Library
  2. Fill in details:
  3. Name: Display name
  4. Description: Optional notes
  5. Folders: Paths to scan (one per line)
  6. Cron Schedule: Auto-scan frequency
  7. Click Save

docs.title: Name
docs.text:
Display name shown in the UI.

Examples: - "Main Collection" - "Manga Library" - "Downloads"

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
Optional field for notes about the library.

Examples: - "Completed series only" - "Auto-imported from downloads folder" - "Shared family collection"

docs.title: Folders
docs.text:
List of absolute paths to scan for manga, one per line.

Linux: 

/home/user/manga
/mnt/nas/manga
/series/external/manga

Windows: 

C:\Manga
D:\Downloads\Manga
\\NAS\Media\Manga

Docker: 

/data/manga
/data/manga/completed
/data/manga/ongoing

[!TIP] Magi recursively scans subdirectories, so you can point to a top-level folder.

docs.title: Cron Schedule
docs.text:
Determines when the library is automatically re-indexed.

Format: minute hour day month weekday

Common schedules:

Schedule Description 0 2 * * * Daily at 2 AM 0 */6 * * * Every 6 hours 0 0 * * 0 Weekly (Sunday midnight) 0 3 * * 1 Weekly (Monday 3 AM) @hourly Every hour @daily Daily at midnight @weekly Weekly (Sunday midnight)

Examples:

  • Light users: 0 3 * * * (daily at 3 AM)
  • Heavy users: 0 */3 * * * (every 3 hours)
  • Weekly updates: 0 2 * * 0 (Sunday 2 AM)

Disable auto-scan: Leave blank or use a very infrequent schedule.

docs.title: Editing
docs.text:
1. Click Edit next to the library
  2. Modify settings
  3. Click Save

docs.title: Manual Indexing
docs.text:
Click Index Now to immediately scan the library, bypassing the schedule.

Use when: - Adding new manga - Reorganizing files - Testing configuration changes

docs.title: Deleting
docs.text:
1. Click Delete next to the library
  2. Confirm deletion

[!WARNING] Deleting a library removes all associated manga and chapters from the database. This does NOT delete your manga files.

docs.title: User Management
docs.text: Manage users from Admin > Users (admin role required).

docs.title: Reader
docs.text:
Permissions: - Browse and search manga - Read chapters - Track reading progress - Add favorites - Vote on manga

Cannot: - Edit metadata - Manage libraries - Create/manage users

docs.title: Moderator
docs.text:
Permissions: - All Reader permissions - Update manga metadata - Refresh manga/chapters - Manual metadata editing - Create/edit scrapers

Cannot: - Manage libraries - Manage users - Access configuration

docs.title: Admin
docs.text: Permissions: - All Moderator permissions - Create/edit/delete libraries - Manage users (create, ban, change roles) - Access configuration page - View all admin features

docs.title: Changing User Roles
docs.text:
1. Navigate to Admin > Users
  2. Find the user
  3. Click Change Role
  4. Select new role
  5. Confirm

docs.title: Banning Users
docs.text:
Banned users cannot log in or access any content.

  1. Navigate to Admin > Users
  2. Find the user
  3. Click Ban
  4. Confirm

To unban: 1. Click Unban next to the user 2. Confirm

docs.title: Creating Users (Admin)
docs.text:
While users can self-register (if enabled), admins can manually create accounts:

  1. Enable registration temporarily
  2. Share the registration link
  3. Let user create account
  4. Disable registration
  5. Assign appropriate role

[!NOTE] Admins cannot directly create user accounts through the UI yet. Users must register themselves.

docs.title: Content Rating Filter
docs.text: Magi respects MangaDex content ratings and can filter content.

docs.title: Content Ratings
docs.text: Rating Description Safe All-ages content Suggestive Mild fanservice Erotica Sexual content (not explicit) Pornographic Explicit sexual content

docs.title: Filtering
docs.text: Currently, content rating filtering is handled at the database level. Future updates may add user-level filtering.

docs.title: Scraper Configuration
docs.text: Magi includes a web-based scraper system for downloading manga from online sources.

docs.title: Accessing Scrapers
docs.text: Navigate to Admin > Scrapers (moderator/admin only).

docs.title: Creating a Scraper
docs.text:
1. Click New Script
  2. Enter script details:
  3. Name: Descriptive name
  4. URL: Target website
  5. Variables: Custom parameters
  6. Write JavaScript code in the editor
  7. Click Save

docs.title: Running Scrapers
docs.text:
1. Select a scraper from the list
  2. Configure variables (if any)
  3. Click Run
  4. Monitor progress in Job Status

docs.title: Scraper Variables
docs.text:
Variables allow dynamic scraper behavior:

Example: 

manga_id = 12345
chapter_start = 1
chapter_end = 100

Access in JavaScript: 

const mangaId = variables.manga_id;

[!WARNING] Scrapers are powerful but can violate website terms of service. Use responsibly and respect copyright laws.

docs.title: Location
docs.text:
The SQLite database is stored in MAGI_DATA_DIR:

$MAGI_DATA_DIR/magi.db

docs.title: Backup
docs.text:
Manual backup:

# Stop Magi
# Copy database file
cp ~/.local/share/magi/magi.db ~/backups/magi-$(date +%Y%m%d).db
# Restart Magi

Automated backup (Linux):

# Add to crontab
0 3 * * * cp ~/.local/share/magi/magi.db ~/backups/magi-$(date +\%Y\%m\%d).db

docs.title: Restore
docs.text:
# Stop Magi
# Replace database
cp ~/backups/magi-20240101.db ~/.local/share/magi/magi.db
# Restart Magi

docs.title: Reset Database
docs.text:
[!DANGER] This deletes ALL data: users, libraries, manga, chapters, progress.

# Stop Magi
rm ~/.local/share/magi/magi.db
# Restart Magi (creates fresh database)

docs.title: Indexing Performance
docs.text:
Factors affecting speed: - Collection size - Internet speed (for metadata) - Disk I/O (especially for cover art) - CPU (for archive extraction)

Tips: - Use SSD storage for faster indexing - Limit concurrent libraries indexing - Schedule indexing during off-peak hours - Use CBZ instead of RAR

docs.title: Memory Usage
docs.text:
Magi is memory-efficient, but large collections may need tuning.

Docker memory limit: 

docker run -d --memory=2g alexbruun/magi:latest

Systemd memory limit: 

[Service]
MemoryMax=2G

docs.title: Cache Management
docs.text:
Magi caches: - Cover images - Chapter thumbnails - Extracted pages (temporary)

Location: $MAGI_DATA_DIR/cache/

Clear cache: 

rm -rf ~/.local/share/magi/cache/*

docs.title: Network Access
docs.text:
By default, Magi listens on all interfaces (0.0.0.0:3000).

Restrict to localhost: Not currently configurable. Use reverse proxy or firewall rules.

docs.title: HTTPS
docs.text:
Magi doesn't include built-in HTTPS. Use a reverse proxy:

  • Nginx: Guide
  • Caddy: Automatic HTTPS with Let's Encrypt
  • Traefik: Container-native reverse proxy

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
Magi uses JWT tokens for authentication:

  • Access tokens expire after 15 minutes
  • Refresh tokens stored in HTTP-only cookies
  • Tokens invalidated on password change

docs.title: Password Security
docs.text:
• Passwords hashed with bcrypt
  • No password requirements (set your own)
  • Change password: Account > Settings (not yet implemented)

docs.title: Multiple Instances
docs.text:
Run multiple Magi instances with different data directories:

Instance 1: 

MAGI_DATA_DIR=/data/magi1 PORT=3000 ./magi

Instance 2: 

MAGI_DATA_DIR=/data/magi2 PORT=3001 ./magi

docs.title: Custom Data Locations
docs.text:
Structure your data directory:

/var/lib/magi/
├── magi.db          # Database
├── cache/           # Cached images
│   ├── covers/
│   └── pages/
└── logs/            # Application logs (if enabled)

docs.title: Nginx
docs.text:
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name magi.example.com;

    location / {
        proxy_pass http://localhost:3000;
        proxy_http_version 1.1;
        proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
        proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade';
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_cache_bypass $http_upgrade;
    }
}

docs.title: Caddy
docs.text:
magi.example.com {
    reverse_proxy localhost:3000
}

docs.title: Traefik (Docker labels)
docs.text:
labels:
  - "traefik.enable=true"
  - "traefik.http.routers.magi.rule=Host(`magi.example.com`)"
  - "traefik.http.services.magi.loadbalancer.server.port=3000"

docs.title: Changes Not Applied
docs.text:
1. Verify settings saved correctly
  2. Restart Magi service/container
  3. Check environment variables are set
  4. Clear browser cache

docs.title: Port Conflicts
docs.text:
If port 3000 is in use:

# Find process using port
sudo lsof -i :3000

# Use different port
PORT=8080 ./magi

docs.title: Permission Issues
docs.text:
Ensure Magi can: - Read manga directories - Write to data directory - Bind to configured port

# Check permissions
ls -la /path/to/manga
ls -la $MAGI_DATA_DIR

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Troubleshoot common issues
  • Learn about web-based scrapers (coming soon)
  • Explore advanced features (coming soon)

docs.title: Getting Started with Magi
docs.text: This guide will walk you through the initial setup and basic usage of Magi after installation.

docs.title: 1. Access Magi
docs.text:
Open your web browser and navigate to:

http://localhost:3000

Or if accessing from another device on your network:

http://[server-ip]:3000

docs.title: 2. Create Your Admin Account
docs.text:
On first launch, you'll see the registration page. Create your account:

  1. Enter a username (alphanumeric, no spaces)
  2. Enter a password (minimum 8 characters recommended)
  3. Confirm your password
  4. Click Register

[!IMPORTANT] The first user to register automatically becomes an administrator with full access to all features.

After registration, you'll be logged in automatically.

docs.title: Creating Your First Library
docs.text: Libraries are collections of manga organized by folder paths. Let's create your first library.

docs.title: 1. Navigate to Libraries
docs.text: Click Admin in the navigation bar, then select Libraries.

docs.title: 2. Create a New Library
docs.text: Click the New Library button.

docs.title: 3. Configure Library Settings
docs.text:
Fill in the library details:

Field Description Example Name Display name for the library My Manga Collection Description Optional description Main manga library Folders Paths to scan (one per line) /data/manga/mnt/nas/manga Cron Schedule When to auto-scan (cron format) 0 2 * * * (2 AM daily)

docs.title: Understanding Cron Schedules
docs.text:
Cron schedules determine when Magi automatically re-scans your library for changes.

Common schedules:

Schedule Meaning When It Runs 0 2 * * * Daily at 2 AM Every day at 2:00 AM 0 */6 * * * Every 6 hours 12 AM, 6 AM, 12 PM, 6 PM 0 0 * * 0 Weekly on Sunday Every Sunday at midnight @hourly Every hour Top of every hour @daily Daily at midnight Every day at 12:00 AM

Cron format: minute hour day month weekday

Use crontab.guru to build custom schedules.

docs.title: 4. Save the Library
docs.text: Click Save to create the library.

docs.title: 5. Start Initial Indexing
docs.text:
After creating the library:

  1. You'll see your new library in the list
  2. Click the Index Now button to start scanning
  3. Monitor progress in the Admin > Job Status page

The indexing process will:

  • Scan all folders for manga files
  • Detect manga by folder/file names
  • Fetch metadata from MangaDex
  • Download cover art
  • Index all chapters

docs.title: Organizing Your Manga Files
docs.text: Magi works best when your manga is organized logically.

docs.title: Recommended Folder Structure
docs.text:
/data/manga/
├── One Piece/
│   ├── Chapter 001.cbz
│   ├── Chapter 002.cbz
│   └── ...
├── Attack on Titan/
│   ├── Volume 01/
│   │   ├── Chapter 001.cbz
│   │   └── Chapter 002.cbz
│   └── Volume 02/
│       └── ...
└── Naruto/
    ├── 001 - Uzumaki Naruto.cbz
    ├── 002 - Konohamaru.cbz
    └── ...

docs.title: Supported Archive Formats
docs.text:
• ✅ CBZ (ZIP-based) - Recommended
  • ✅ CBR (RAR-based)
  • ✅ ZIP files
  • ✅ RAR files

docs.title: File Naming Tips
docs.text:
Magi is smart about parsing manga names from folders and files:

Good naming: - Manga Name/Chapter 001.cbz - [Author] Manga Name/Vol.01 Ch.001.cbz - Series Title (2020)/Chapter 001 - Title.cbz

What Magi ignores: - Brackets: [Group], (Year) - Common patterns: Vol., Ch., Chapter, etc. - File extensions

Single-file manga:

You can also have manga as individual files: 

/data/manga/
├── One Shot Title.cbz
├── Short Story.cbz

docs.title: Home Page
docs.text:
The home page shows:

  • Recently Added: Latest manga in your libraries
  • Popular: Most favorited manga
  • Statistics: Total manga, chapters, and users

docs.title: Mangas Page
docs.text:
Click Mangas in the navigation to browse your full collection.

Features:

  • Search: Type to filter by title or author
  • Sort: By name, date added, favorites
  • Filter: By library, tags, content rating, type
  • Tags: Select multiple tags to narrow results

docs.title: Reading Manga
docs.text:
1. Click on a manga cover to view details
  2. Browse the chapter list
  3. Click a chapter to start reading

docs.title: Reading Modes
docs.text: Magi offers three reading modes:

docs.title: Webtoon Mode
docs.text:
• Vertical scrolling
  • All pages loaded in sequence
  • Best for: Webtoons and continuous reading
  • Shortcut: Select "Webtoon" in reader toolbar

docs.title: Single Page Mode
docs.text:
• One page at a time
  • Navigate with arrows or keyboard
  • Best for: Traditional manga
  • Shortcuts:
  • → or Space: Next page
  • ←: Previous page

docs.title: Side-by-Side Mode
docs.text:
• Two pages displayed together
  • Mimics physical manga reading
  • Best for: Double-page spreads
  • Navigation: Same as single page mode

docs.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
docs.text: Key Action → Next page ← Previous page Space Next page Home First page End Last page Esc Exit reader

docs.title: Favorites
docs.text:
Mark manga as favorites:

  1. Open a manga page
  2. Click the heart icon ★
  3. Access your favorites from the Account menu

docs.title: Reading Progress
docs.text:
Magi automatically tracks:

  • Which chapters you've read
  • Your current position in each manga
  • Recently read manga

View your reading list: Account > Reading

docs.title: Voting
docs.text:
Show your preferences by voting:

  • Upvote ▲: Manga you love
  • Downvote ▼: Manga you dislike

This helps you organize your collection and can influence future features.

docs.title: Automatic Metadata
docs.text:
When indexing, Magi automatically:

  1. Searches MangaDex for matching manga
  2. Downloads metadata (title, author, description, tags)
  3. Fetches cover art
  4. Applies content rating and status

docs.title: Manual Metadata Editing
docs.text:
If automatic metadata is incorrect:

  1. Open the manga page
  2. Click Update Metadata (moderator/admin only)
  3. Choose an option:
  4. Search MangaDex: Find and apply different metadata
  5. Manual Edit: Enter custom information
  6. Refresh: Re-scan the folder for new chapters

docs.title: Refresh vs Re-Index
docs.text:
• Refresh: Detects new chapters, keeps existing metadata
  • Re-Index: Completely rescans, refetches metadata

docs.title: User Roles
docs.text:
Magi has three user roles:

Role Permissions Reader Browse, read, track progress, favorite Moderator Reader + edit metadata, manage manga Admin Moderator + manage users, libraries, configuration

Admins can change user roles from Admin > Users.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
Now that you're set up, explore these features:

  • Configure advanced settings
  • Set up user accounts for family/friends
  • Troubleshoot common issues
  • Learn about web-based scrapers (coming soon)

docs.title: Performance
docs.text:
• Use CBZ/ZIP instead of RAR for better performance
  • Store manga on fast storage (SSD if possible)
  • For large collections, increase indexing intervals

docs.title: Organization
docs.text:
• Use consistent naming conventions
  • Group manga by series in folders
  • Consider separating completed vs ongoing series
  • Tag manga appropriately for easy filtering

docs.title: Maintenance
docs.text:
• Regularly check Job Status for indexing issues
  • Monitor disk space usage
  • Back up your database periodically
  • Update Magi to get new features and fixes

docs.title: Common Questions
docs.text:
Q: How long does initial indexing take? A: Depends on collection size. Roughly 1-2 seconds per manga. A 1000-manga library takes 15-30 minutes.

Q: Can I have multiple libraries? A: Yes! Create as many as you need for different collections.

Q: What if MangaDex doesn't have my manga? A: Use manual metadata editing to add custom information.

Q: Can I import my existing metadata? A: Currently, Magi fetches fresh metadata. Manual editing is available.

Q: How do I add more users? A: They can register themselves (if enabled) or admins can create accounts.

Q: Is there a mobile app? A: Not yet, but the web interface is mobile-responsive.

docs.title: Getting Help
docs.text:
• Documentation: You're reading it!
  • GitHub Issues: Report bugs or request features
  • Discussions: Ask questions and share ideas

Enjoy reading your manga with Magi! 📚✨

docs.title: Troubleshooting Guide
docs.text: This guide covers common issues and their solutions when running Magi.

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• Installation Issues
  • Database Problems
  • Indexing Issues
  • Reading and Display Issues
  • Performance Problems
  • Network and Access Issues
  • User and Authentication Issues
  • Advanced Debugging

docs.title: Binary Won't Run
docs.text:
Symptoms: Permission denied or command not found

Solution: 

# Make binary executable
chmod +x magi

# Verify it's executable
ls -la magi

# Run with explicit path
./magi

docs.title: Port Already in Use
docs.text:
Symptoms: Error: listen tcp :3000: bind: address already in use

Solution:

Find what's using the port: 

# Linux
sudo lsof -i :3000
sudo ss -tulpn | grep 3000

# Windows (PowerShell)
netstat -ano | findstr :3000

Change Magi's port: 

PORT=8080 ./magi

docs.title: Missing Dependencies
docs.text:
Symptoms: error while loading shared libraries

Solution:

Linux: 

# Install common dependencies
sudo apt install libsqlite3-0  # Debian/Ubuntu
sudo dnf install sqlite-libs   # Fedora/RHEL

Windows: Install Microsoft Visual C++ Redistributable

docs.title: Database Locked
docs.text:
Symptoms: database is locked errors in logs

Cause: Multiple Magi instances accessing the same database

Solution: 1. Stop all Magi instances 2. Ensure only one instance runs per database 3. Check for orphaned processes: ps aux | grep magi

docs.title: Corrupted Database
docs.text:
Symptoms: database disk image is malformed, crashes on startup

Solution:

Check integrity: 

sqlite3 ~/magi/magi.db "PRAGMA integrity_check;"

Attempt repair: 

sqlite3 ~/magi/magi.db ".recover" | sqlite3 ~/magi/magi_recovered.db

Restore from backup: 

# Stop Magi
cp ~/backups/magi.db ~/.local/share/magi/magi.db
# Start Magi

Last resort - Reset database (⚠️ Deletes all data): 

rm ~/.local/share/magi/magi.db

docs.title: Database Inspection
docs.text:
Access database: 

# Install SQLite tools
sudo apt install sqlite3  # Debian/Ubuntu
sudo dnf install sqlite   # Fedora/RHEL

# Open database
sqlite3 ~/magi/magi.db

Common queries: 

-- View tables
.tables

-- View schema
.schema

-- Count manga
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM mangas;

-- Count users
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users;

-- View all users
SELECT username, role, banned FROM users;

-- Check configuration
SELECT * FROM app_config;

-- Exit
.quit

docs.title: Library Won't Index
docs.text:
Symptoms: Library shows 0 manga, indexing completes but nothing added

Causes and solutions:

docs.title: 1. Incorrect Path
docs.text:
# Verify path exists
ls -la /path/to/manga

# Check Docker volume mounts
docker inspect magi | grep Mounts -A 20

docs.title: 2. Permission Denied
docs.text:
# Check file permissions
ls -la /path/to/manga

# Fix permissions (Linux)
chmod -R 755 /path/to/manga
chown -R magi:magi /path/to/manga

# Docker: ensure volume is readable
docker run --rm -v /path/to/manga:/data:ro busybox ls -la /data

docs.title: 3. No Supported Files
docs.text:
Magi requires: - CBZ, CBR, ZIP, or RAR files - Or directories containing images

Verify structure: 

/manga/
├── Series Name/          # Folder with manga name
│   ├── Chapter 1.cbz    # Chapter files
│   └── Chapter 2.cbz

docs.title: 4. Invalid File Names
docs.text:
Problematic names: - Non-UTF-8 characters - Very long filenames - Special characters: <>:"|?*

Fix: 

# Rename files with problematic characters
find /path/to/manga -name '*[<>:"|?*]*' -print

docs.title: Manga Not Found on MangaDex
docs.text:
Symptoms: Manga indexed but no metadata or cover art

Solutions:

  1. Manual metadata update:
  2. Open manga page
  3. Click Update Metadata (moderator/admin)
  4. Search for correct manga

  5. Apply metadata

  6. Manual edit:

  7. Click Manual Edit
  8. Enter title, author, description manually

  9. Save

  10. Offline mode: Magi uses folder names if no metadata found. This is normal for:

  11. Unlicensed manga
  12. Very new releases
  13. Non-English manga

docs.title: Slow Indexing
docs.text:
Symptoms: Indexing takes hours for moderate collections

Causes and solutions:

docs.title: 1. Slow Internet
docs.text:
Metadata fetching requires internet. Each manga queries MangaDex API.

Solution: Be patient or index during off-peak hours.

docs.title: 2. Slow Disk I/O
docs.text:
Cover art downloads and archive extraction are disk-intensive.

Solution: - Use SSD storage - Reduce concurrent operations - Index one library at a time

docs.title: 3. Large RAR Archives
docs.text:
RAR files are slow for random access.

Solution: Convert to CBZ: 

# Extract and recompress as ZIP
for f in *.cbr; do
    mkdir temp
    unrar x "$f" temp/
    cd temp
    zip -r "../${f%.cbr}.cbz" *
    cd ..
    rm -rf temp
done

docs.title: Duplicate Detection Issues
docs.text:
Symptoms: Same manga indexed multiple times

Cause: Multiple folders with similar names, or same files in different libraries

Solution:

  1. Check Admin > Duplicates (if available)
  2. Manually delete unwanted duplicates:
  3. Open duplicate manga
  4. Click Delete (admin only)
  5. Reorganize folders to avoid duplicates

docs.title: Images Not Loading
docs.text:
Symptoms: Blank pages, broken images in reader

Causes and solutions:

docs.title: 1. Archive Extraction Failed
docs.text:
Check logs: 

# Docker
docker logs magi

# Systemd
journalctl -u magi -f

# Direct run
# Check terminal output

Solution: Re-index manga to retry extraction.

docs.title: 2. Cached Data Corruption
docs.text:
Clear cache: 

# Stop Magi
rm -rf ~/.local/share/magi/cache/*
# Start Magi

docs.title: 3. File Permissions
docs.text:
Verify Magi can read files: 

ls -la /path/to/manga/SeriesName/Chapter.cbz

# Test extraction manually
unzip -t /path/to/manga/SeriesName/Chapter.cbz

docs.title: Reader Controls Not Working
docs.text:
Symptoms: Keyboard shortcuts don't work, navigation broken

Solutions:

  1. Reload page: Press F5 or Ctrl+R
  2. Clear browser cache: Ctrl+Shift+Delete
  3. Check JavaScript: Ensure JavaScript is enabled
  4. Try different browser: Test in Chrome/Firefox

docs.title: Cover Art Missing
docs.text:
Symptoms: Manga has no cover image, shows placeholder

Causes:

  1. MangaDex API issue: Try updating metadata
  2. Network error during download: Re-index manga
  3. Cache cleared: Will re-download on next access

Solution: 

# Force cover re-download
# Option 1: Re-index manga
Click "Refresh Metadata" on manga page

# Option 2: Delete cached cover
rm ~/.local/share/magi/cache/covers/manga-slug.jpg

docs.title: Wrong Chapter Order
docs.text:
Symptoms: Chapters listed in incorrect order

Cause: Chapter naming doesn't sort naturally

Examples of problematic naming: - Chapter 1, Chapter 10, Chapter 2 (string sort) - Mixed formats: Ch 1, Chapter 2, Ep 3

Solution:

Rename chapters with zero-padded numbers: 

# Example: Rename to 001, 002, etc.
for i in {1..99}; do
    mv "Chapter $i.cbz" "Chapter $(printf %03d $i).cbz"
done

docs.title: High Memory Usage
docs.text:
Symptoms: Magi using 2GB+ RAM, system slowdown

Solutions:

Docker - Limit memory: 

docker run -d --memory=1g --memory-swap=2g alexbruun/magi:latest

Systemd - Limit memory: 

[Service]
MemoryMax=1G
MemoryHigh=800M

Reduce collection size: - Split into multiple libraries - Index fewer manga at once

docs.title: Slow Page Loading
docs.text:
Symptoms: Pages take seconds to load in reader

Causes and solutions:

docs.title: 1. Large Image Files
docs.text:
Some manga have huge images (10MB+ per page).

No direct solution, but Magi should cache after first load.

docs.title: 2. Slow Storage
docs.text: Solution: Move manga to faster storage (SSD).

docs.title: 3. Network Latency
docs.text:
If accessing Magi over network, latency affects load times.

Solution: Use local network, not internet.

docs.title: High CPU Usage
docs.text:
Symptoms: 100% CPU during indexing

This is normal during: - Initial indexing - Cover art download/processing - Archive extraction

If persistent after indexing: 1. Check for indexing loops (bad cron schedule) 2. Verify no corrupted archives causing retries 3. Restart Magi

docs.title: Can't Access from Other Devices
docs.text:
Symptoms: http://[server-ip]:3000 times out from other computers

Solutions:

docs.title: 1. Firewall Blocking Port
docs.text:
Linux (firewalld): 

sudo firewall-cmd --add-port=3000/tcp --permanent
sudo firewall-cmd --reload

Linux (ufw): 

sudo ufw allow 3000/tcp
sudo ufw reload

Windows: 

New-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName "Magi" -Direction Inbound -Protocol TCP -LocalPort 3000 -Action Allow

docs.title: 2. Wrong IP Address
docs.text:
Find correct IP: 

# Linux
ip addr show
ifconfig

# Windows
ipconfig

Use the IP from your local network (usually 192.168.x.x or 10.x.x.x).

docs.title: 3. Docker Network Mode
docs.text: If using Docker's host network mode, IP should be the host's IP, not Docker's.

docs.title: CORS Errors
docs.text:
Symptoms: Browser console shows CORS policy errors

Cause: Accessing Magi from different domain/port

Solution: Use reverse proxy (Nginx, Caddy) with proper CORS headers.

docs.title: WebSocket Connection Failed
docs.text:
Symptoms: Job status not updating in real-time

Cause: Reverse proxy not forwarding WebSocket connections

Solution (Nginx): 

location / {
    proxy_pass http://localhost:3000;
    proxy_http_version 1.1;
    proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
    proxy_set_header Connection "upgrade";
}

docs.title: Can't Login
docs.text:
Symptoms: "Invalid username or password" despite correct credentials

Solutions:

docs.title: 1. Password Typo
docs.text: Double-check username and password (case-sensitive).

docs.title: 2. Account Banned
docs.text: Ask admin to check: Admin > Users

docs.title: 3. Database Issue
docs.text:
# Check if user exists
sqlite3 ~/magi/magi.db "SELECT username, banned FROM users WHERE username='yourname';"

docs.title: Logged Out Repeatedly
docs.text:
Symptoms: Session expires too quickly

Cause: Tokens expire after 15 minutes (by design)

Workaround: Stay active or login again.

docs.title: Registration Disabled
docs.text:
Symptoms: "Registration is disabled" message

Cause: Admin disabled registration in config

Solution: Ask admin to enable registration temporarily, or have admin change your role.

docs.title: Can't Change Password
docs.text:
Symptoms: No password change option in UI

Current limitation: Password changes not yet implemented in UI.

Workaround (admin): 

# Manually reset password via database
# (Requires bcrypt hash generation - complex)

docs.title: Enable Debug Logging
docs.text:
Linux: 

# Not currently configurable
# Check implementation for log level options

Docker: 

docker logs -f magi

Systemd: 

journalctl -u magi -f -n 100

docs.title: Check Application Health
docs.text:
Test HTTP endpoint: 

curl -v http://localhost:3000/

Expected: HTML response with status 200.

docs.title: Verify File System
docs.text:
Check file system for errors: 

# Linux - Check disk
sudo fsck /dev/sda1

# Check manga directory
ls -laR /path/to/manga | head -100

docs.title: Network Debugging
docs.text:
Test connectivity: 

# From same server
curl http://localhost:3000

# From another device
curl http://[server-ip]:3000

# Test DNS
nslookup magi.example.com

docs.title: Database Debugging
docs.text:
Check database size: 

du -h ~/.local/share/magi/magi.db

Analyze query performance: 

sqlite3 ~/magi/magi.db
> EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN SELECT * FROM mangas;

Vacuum database: 

sqlite3 ~/magi/magi.db "VACUUM;"

docs.title: Container Debugging
docs.text:
Docker: 

# Shell into container
docker exec -it magi /bin/sh

# Check environment
docker exec magi env

# Check processes
docker top magi

# Inspect container config
docker inspect magi

Podman: 

# Similar commands, replace docker with podman
podman exec -it magi /bin/sh

docs.title: Getting Help
docs.text:
If you're still stuck:

  1. Check existing issues: GitHub Issues
  2. Search discussions: GitHub Discussions
  3. Create new issue: Include:
  4. Magi version (./magi --version)
  5. Operating system
  6. Installation method (binary, Docker, etc.)
  7. Relevant logs
  8. Steps to reproduce
  9. Ask questions: Post in Discussions for general help

docs.title: Collecting Debug Information
docs.text:
When reporting issues, provide:

# Magi version
./magi --version

# OS info
uname -a

# Docker version (if using Docker)
docker --version

# Recent logs (last 100 lines)
journalctl -u magi -n 100 --no-pager

# Database stats
sqlite3 ~/magi/magi.db "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM mangas; SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users;"

# Disk space
df -h

docs.title: database schema version mismatch
docs.text: Solution: Database needs migration. Restart Magi (migrations run automatically).

docs.title: failed to fetch metadata: connection refused
docs.text: Solution: - Check internet connection - MangaDex may be down, try again later - Firewall blocking outbound HTTPS

docs.title: failed to extract archive: unsupported format
docs.text: Solution: - Archive may be corrupted - Unsupported compression method - Re-download or convert to standard CBZ

docs.title: permission denied: /data/magi/magi.db
docs.text: Solution: Fix file permissions (see Permission Errors above).

docs.title: context deadline exceeded
docs.text: Solution: - Request timed out (slow network/API) - Try again - Increase timeout (not currently configurable)

docs.title: Preventive Maintenance
docs.text:
Avoid issues with regular maintenance:

  1. Backup database weekly: 

    cp ~/.local/share/magi/magi.db ~/backups/

  2. Monitor disk space: 

    df -h

  3. Update Magi regularly: Stay on latest version for bug fixes.

  4. Organize files properly: Consistent naming and structure reduces issues.

  5. Test after changes: After configuration changes, verify everything works.

docs.title: Known Issues
docs.text:
Track known issues and their status: GitHub Issues

Common known issues: - RAR archives slower than ZIP (by design) - Large collections (10,000+ manga) may be slow - Password change UI not yet implemented

Still having problems? Open an issue with detailed information, and the community will help!
